topic: Zed AI Editor Guide 2026: ACP Protocol, AI Features, and Performance

competitors.title: Zed Editor Review 2026: Fast Enough to Switch?
key_points: Zed is the fastest code editor in 2026: 0.4s startup vs VS Code 3.0s, 180MB idle RAM vs 650MB, 2ms input latency vs 12ms
AI suggestions appear in under 80ms — faster than Cursor (150ms) or VS Code + Copilot (160ms)
Zed AI costs $10/month for hosted Claude-powered assistant; BYO API key is free; team plan $20/user/month
Zed AI strengths: speed, file-level assistance, BYO-key, inline edits; weaknesses: no codebase indexing, no multi-file edits like Cursor Composer, no agentic capabilities like Windsurf Cascade
Overall rating 8.7/10 (Speed 9.8, AI 8.0, Ecosystem 7.5, Collaboration 9.5)
~800 extensions vs VS Code's 50,000+
Best for developers who value performance and collaboration; stick with VS Code/Cursor if you need specialized extensions or codebase-wide AI

competitors.title: Zed IDE Complete Guide 2026: The Rust-Powered Editor Changing the Future of Development
key_points: Zed founded by Nathan Sobo (Atom creator) in 2021, uses Rust + GPUI (GPU-accelerated rendering) for 120fps editing
Performance benchmarks: 0.12s cold startup vs VS Code 1.2s (10x faster), large project 0.25s vs 3.8s (15x faster), 50MB file 0.8s vs 3.2s (4x faster)
Zed AI's 4 core features: Agentic Editing (natural language multi-file edits at 120fps), Edit Prediction (Zeta model), Inline Assistant, Text Threads (plain text LLM interface)
ACP announced January 2026, collaboration with JetBrains to standardize agent ecosystem — open protocol, Apache license
Supports Claude Sonnet 4/4.6, OpenAI GPT-4/GPT-4 Turbo, Anthropic Claude 3 Opus/Sonnet/Haiku, Google Gemini 3 Flash, DeepSeek, Ollama/LM Studio (local LLMs)
Real-time collaboration based on CRDT technology (same as Google Docs/Figma) — cursor sharing, voice chat, screen sharing, shared terminal sessions, channel-based project rooms
Limitations: ~500+ extensions (not 50K+ like VS Code), Python requires manual setup (basedpyright + Ruff), limited DAP debugging, no settings sync, some AI features paywalled

competitors.title: Zed — Agent Client Protocol (ACP)
key_points: ACP is an open standard (Apache license) enabling any AI agent to integrate with any editing environment — no vendor lock-in

Editors on ACP: Zed (native), JetBrains IDEs (in progress), Neovim (via CodeCompanion/avante.nvim plugins), Emacs (via agent-shell), marimo, AionUI, Obsidian (via plugin), Sidequery (in progress), Web Browser (AI SDK)
Agents on ACP: Claude Agent, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, Codex CLI, Cursor, Cline, Mistral Vibe, OpenCode, Goose (Square), Augment Code, Blackbox AI, Kimi CLI, Qwen Code, and more (20+ agents total)

ACP core principles: Universal Compatibility (any agent on ACP gains access to multi-file editing, codebase context, reviewing tools), Privacy First (third-party agents never touch Zed servers), Open Source
Key milestones: Aug 2025 (Gemini CLI as first integration), Sep 2025 (Claude Code), Oct 2025 (JetBrains partnership, Codex integration), Jan 2026 (ACP announced with JetBrains collaboration)

competitors.title: Agent Client Protocol (ACP): Use Any Coding Agent in Any IDE — JetBrains
key_points: ACP co-developed by JetBrains and Zed as 'the industry standard for connecting agents and editors'
ACP eliminates one-off integrations — shared protocol means editors and agents connect through the same interface
Supports local, remote, and in-house agents — enterprises can connect custom-built agents on their own infrastructure
Major agents already on ACP: Junie (JetBrains), Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Cursor, Mistral Vibe, OpenCode, Kimi CLI, Qwen Code, Cline, Kiro CLI
Quote from Mistral AI: 'ACP has been a real joy to use: thoughtfully designed, community-driven, and evolving rapidly'
Quote from Augment Code: 'Developers should be able to use the best AI agent in the workflow that suits them, and ACP makes that possible'
March 2026: Cursor joined ACP Registry and is now available in JetBrains IDEs

stats.fact: Zed cold startup: 0.12-0.4s vs VS Code 1.2-3.0s (10x faster)
Zed idle RAM: 150-250MB vs VS Code 300-650MB (2-4x less)
Zed input latency: 2ms vs VS Code/Cursor 12-25ms
Zed AI suggestions appear in <80ms vs Cursor 150ms vs VS Code+Copilot 160ms
Zed GPU-accelerated rendering achieves 120fps editing via custom GPUI framework
ACP supports 20+ AI agents including Claude Agent, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Cursor, Mistral Vibe, Cline, Goose
stats.source: Zed ACP page / JetBrains ACP page

stats.fact: ACP supports 10+ editors/clients: Zed (native), JetBrains IDEs (in progress), Neovim, Emacs, marimo, AionUI, Obsidian, Web Browser
Zed AI pricing: $10/month individual, $20/user/month team, BYO API key free
JetBrains and Zed co-developed ACP as open-source Apache-licensed standard for IDE-agent interoperability
Zed has ~500-800 extensions vs VS Code's 50,000+
Zed founded 2021 by Nathan Sobo (Atom creator); Windows support launched Jan 2025; ACP announced Jan 2026
Cursor joined ACP Registry March 2026 — available as agent inside JetBrains IDEs

angles: Zed as the first 'Agentic IDE' — AI agents are not bolted on, they're integrated via ACP into the editor's DNA
ACP as the game-changer for developer freedom — no vendor lock-in, choose any agent in any editor
Performance-first development: why 0.12s startup and 2ms latency matter for developer experience and flow state
The JetBrains-Zed partnership: how two competitors are building an open standard that challenges VS Code dominance
Zed AI vs Cursor vs VS Code: when to choose each — performance vs ecosystem vs codebase-wide AI
Local LLM privacy advantage: Ollama/LM Studio support means code never leaves your machine
Collaboration built-in: CRDT-based real-time editing (like Figma for code) vs VS Code Live Share
